new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage of heading 2208 that is a liqueur, i.e. a spirit drink produced from ethyl alcohol of agricultural origin or a distillate, or one or more spirits, flavoured by means of natural or nature-identical flavouring substances and having a minimum sugar content; see HS Explanatory Notes to heading 2208, first paragraph, letter B and third paragraph.
Liqueur (CN 2208 70) presented in containers with a nominal volume of 2 litres or less, classified under CN code 2208 70 10.
A quality borne by a liqueur that represents its minimum sugar content, expressed as grams of inverted sugar per litre, distinguishing liqueurs from unsweetened spirits under CN heading 2208.

coverage_explanation: The legal note for 22087010 refers to HS Explanatory Notes heading 2208, first paragraph letter B and third paragraph, which describe liqueurs as spirituous beverages produced from ethyl alcohol, distillates or spirits, flavoured with natural or nature-identical flavouring substances, and sweetened (minimum sugar content). The proposed axioms capture: (1) a new Liqueur class as a subclass of SpiritousBeverage2208; (2) a LiqueurSmallContainer22087010 subclass restricted to SmallContainer (≤ 2 L); (3) a SweeteningQuality to represent the mandatory minimum sugar content; (4) restrictions linking Liqueur to FlavouringSubstance (via hasAddedFlavouring) and to SugarOrSweeteningMatter (via hasBaseIngredient), and to SweeteningQuality (via RO:has_quality). Coverage gaps: the precise numeric minimum sugar content threshold (typically 100 g/L inverted sugar under EU Regulation 2019/787) cannot be fully modelled without a dedicated data property for sugar content; the distinction between natural and nature-identical flavouring substances is not formally encoded; the specific base spirit types (agricultural ethyl alcohol, distillate, spirit) are only partially captured through the parent class; the sibling code 22087090 (containers > 2 L) is not modelled here as it is a separate CN code. These gaps reduce coverage to approximately 0.72.
